title: [FSM] Canonicalize away trivial states
body:
Trivial states occur when a state has an always taken transition, there is no output in the state, and the always taken transition has no action associated with it.

This is a fairly restricted optimization that only occurs on FSMs with no output. Once we implement some form of equivalence checking capability for action-, output, and guard regions, this optimization can be subsumed by a more formal state minimization pass.
